---
title: "QIIME2 Metabarcoding Workflow"
author: "GitHub Repository"
output: html_document
---

# Overview

This repository contains a QIIME2 workflow for processing paired-end metabarcoding data, including:

- Importing demultiplexed FASTQ files
- Primer trimming using Cutadapt
- Quality filtering and denoising with DADA2
- Taxonomic assignment
- Phylogenetic tree construction
- Diversity analysis
- Exporting outputs for downstream analysis in R

# References

This workflow was adapted from:

1. QIIME2 Moving Pictures Tutorial  
https://docs.qiime2.org/2019.7/tutorials/moving-pictures/

2. Australian BioCommons Workshop  
https://zenodo.org/records/6350808


# Software Requirements

## QIIME2

Recommended version:

```bash
conda activate qiime2-2021.8
```


## Demultiplexed fast files for this project are available at the NCBI's Sequence Read Archive Bioproject Accession:BioProjectID: PRJNA1432314

# Workflow Summary

## 1. Rename FASTQ Files

Files must follow Casava 1.8 naming conventions:


## 2. Import Data

```bash
qiime tools import
```

## 3. Trim Primers

```bash
qiime cutadapt trim-paired
```

## 4. Assess Sequence Quality

```bash
qiime demux summarize
```

Visualize `.qzv` outputs using:

https://view.qiime2.org

## 5. Denoise Sequences

```bash
qiime dada2 denoise-paired
```

## 6. Assign Taxonomy

```bash
qiime feature-classifier classify-sklearn
```

## 7. Build Phylogenetic Tree

- MAFFT alignment
- Mask alignment
- FastTree phylogeny
- Midpoint rooting

## 8. Diversity Analyses

```bash
qiime diversity alpha-rarefaction
```

## 9. Export Data for R

Outputs exported include:

- Feature table (`feature-table.tsv`)
- Taxonomy table
- Phylogenetic tree (`tree.nwk`)


# Notes

- Adjusted trimming parameters based on quality plots.
- Primer sequences are target-specific will need to change if processing ITS2 or 16S.
- DADA2 runtime depends on dataset size and computational resources.
- The workflow assumes paired-end sequencing data.

```
